input.instructions: Task: Write a Python script (main.py) to solve an optimization problem. Input: N (board size, 9x9), M (number of 3x3 stamps, 20), K (max placements, 81). An initial 9x9 board of integers. M different 3x3 stamps. Objective: Place up to K stamps on the board. Each placement is defined by (stamp_index m, top-left position i, j). When a stamp is placed, its values are added to the corresponding board cells modulo 998244353. Maximize the sum of all final board entries. Output Format: Line 1: L (number of placements, 0 <= L <= K). Next L lines: m i j (stamp index, top-left row, top-left column). Constraints: Board is 9x9. Stamps are 3x3. All placements must keep the 3x3 stamp within the 9x9 board boundaries. Modulo arithmetic (998244353) applies to cell values during addition. Goal: Implement an algorithm (e.g., greedy, hill climbing, or simulated annealing) to find a sequence of placements that maximizes the sum of the final board values. A valid baseline is to output 0 (no placements).

run_level_defaults.selected_prompt_text:
Try to simplify this question down to its core parts, to help the next agent solve it.
Your output should be sufficient for another agent to get, and then produce the final answer.
Use plain text only inside the message. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or LaTeX-style backslashes.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "transmute",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "message_for_next_agent": "plain-text reformulation for the next agent"
}
run_level_defaults.child_selected_prompt_text:
Solve the question directly.
Keep the entire JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Do not narrate your reasoning outside the response field.
In the response field, use exactly two short lines encoded with \n:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>\nJUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>",
  "result_type": "summary"
}
